skill.name: copilot-coding-agent
skill.description: Automates GitHub Copilot Coding Agent assignment from GitHub issues using labels, GitHub Actions, GraphQL, and the GitHub CLI.
skill.summary: Turn labeled GitHub issues into Copilot Coding Agent draft pull requests.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static external-command findings are mostly expected for a GitHub automation skill, but they are real operational risk because the skill instructs Bash, GitHub CLI, and local script execution with a repo-scoped PAT. The weak-cryptography findings appear to be false positives from ordinary text, and the hardcoded URLs are documentation links. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious exfiltration was found.

medium_findings.title: Repository Automation Requires Powerful GitHub Credentials
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to use a repo-scoped PAT and commands that create issues, edit labels, inspect PRs, and assign Copilot. This is legitimate for the stated workflow, but misuse or compromised prompts could modify repository state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly requires a repo-scoped PAT and shows GitHub CLI commands that mutate issues and repository automation state. The behavior matches the skill purpose, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Local Shell Script Execution Referenced But Scripts Are Not Included
medium_findings.description: The skill directs users to run setup and assignment scripts from a scripts directory. Those scripts are not present in the audited file structure, so their behavior cannot be verified from this package.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: There is direct evidence of bash script execution instructions, but the referenced scripts are absent from the scanned package. This prevents confirming whether the script contents are safe.

medium_findings.title: Bulk Issue Mutation Through xargs
medium_findings.description: The backlog example pipes issue numbers into xargs to apply the Copilot label in bulk. This can be valid for triage, but it can also unintentionally delegate many issues to Copilot if run without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The xargs command is explicit and mutates GitHub issues in bulk. The input comes from gh issue list rather than arbitrary user input, which lowers command-injection concern but not operational risk.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Are Benign Network References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to GitHub and VS Code documentation. They do not perform network requests or transmit local data from the skill package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are plain Markdown references to official documentation pages. No code path sends secrets or repository data to those URLs.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography alerts occur in prose and metadata, not in cryptographic implementation. No hashing, encryption, or security algorithm code was found in the audited files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptions, use cases, or command examples rather than crypto APIs. The scanner likely matched substrings such as DES inside ordinary words.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Script Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs users to run bash scripts that set up workflows, secrets, labels, and Copilot assignment. Script execution should be reviewed before use, especially because scripts are not included in this package.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The bash invocations are explicit and tied to repository automation. Missing script contents limit confidence about final behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: GitHub CLI State Changes
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses gh commands to create issues, edit labels, list pull requests, and check CI. These are expected capabilities but can change repository workflow state.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are concrete and repository-scoped. They are not hidden or obfuscated, but they require appropriate authorization boundaries.

content.user_title: Automate GitHub Issues into Copilot Draft PRs
content.value_statement: Teams often lose time moving clear GitHub issues into implementation work. This skill shows a label-based workflow that assigns GitHub Copilot Coding Agent and tracks the resulting draft pull request.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to trigger Copilot Coding Agent with an ai-copilot issue label.
Documents GitHub Actions and GraphQL flow for assigning Copilot to an issue.
Provides GitHub CLI examples for creating issues, adding labels, and checking pull requests.
Lists prerequisites for GitHub plans, Copilot agent enablement, gh authentication, and token scope.
Describes manual approval and CI expectations for Copilot-created draft pull requests.
limitations: Requires a supported GitHub Copilot plan and repository settings enabled for Coding Agent.
Uses a repo-scoped personal access token, which must be protected and reviewed carefully.
References setup scripts that are not included in the audited package.
Copilot output quality depends on the issue specification and still needs human pull request review.

use_cases.title: Product backlog delegation
use_cases.description: Convert approved product issues into Copilot draft pull requests by applying a single label.

use_cases.title: Engineering maintenance queue
use_cases.description: Route refactor, documentation, and test tasks to Copilot while engineers focus on review and integration.

use_cases.title: External ticket automation
use_cases.description: Connect Jira or webhook-created GitHub issues to a label-based Copilot implementation pipeline.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Copilot-ready issue
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a single task
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a clear GitHub issue title and body for this task. Include acceptance criteria that Copilot Coding Agent can implement.

prompt_templates.title: Label an existing issue
prompt_templates.scenario: Assign one reviewed issue to Copilot
prompt_templates.prompt: Check issue NUMBER for readiness. If it has clear scope and tests, show the command to add the ai-copilot label.

prompt_templates.title: Review Copilot draft PR status
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the Copilot-created draft PR for issue NUMBER. Summarize CI status, reviewer actions, and any blocking comments.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a safe backlog batch
prompt_templates.prompt: Select low-risk backlog issues suitable for Copilot assignment. Exclude security, architecture, and ambiguous tasks, then propose a reviewed batch plan.

output_examples.input: A product manager asks to turn a validated feature request into Copilot work.
output_examples.output: The assistant drafts a focused issue with acceptance criteria, recommends the ai-copilot label, and notes that the draft PR needs review.

output_examples.input: An engineering lead wants to process tech debt issues safely.
output: A review step before assigning Copilot.

output_examples.input: A developer asks whether Copilot finished an assigned issue.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks related pull requests, summarizes CI results, and identifies the next human approval step.

best_practices: Review every issue for clear scope, tests, and acceptance criteria before adding the ai-copilot label.
Use the least privileged token that supports the required GitHub Actions and assignment flow.
Require human review for Copilot draft pull requests before merging or enabling follow-up automation.
anti_patterns: Do not bulk-label ambiguous, security-sensitive, or architecture-heavy issues without manual review.
Do not expose the Copilot assignment token in issue text, logs, prompts, or pull request comments.
Do not treat Copilot-created draft pull requests as merge-ready without CI and code review.

faq.question: What does this skill automate?
faq.answer: It documents a GitHub workflow where an issue label assigns Copilot Coding Agent and starts a draft pull request.

faq.question: Does it include the setup scripts?
faq.answer: No. The audited package references setup scripts, but those script files are not included in the file structure.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: It uses Bash, GitHub CLI, and a repo-scoped token to change repository automation state.

faq.question: Is there evidence of malware?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed files contain workflow instructions, not hidden code or data exfiltration logic.

faq.question: What should users verify before running it?
faq.answer: Users should inspect any referenced scripts, confirm token scope, and review every issue before assigning Copilot.
